contents:
Oxidation
Chromium-rich oxide scales can slow oxygen ingress at high temperature.

Creep
Creep rate increases when temperature and applied stress increase together.

Fatigue
Crack initiation often begins at surface defects under cyclic loading.

Heat treatment
Solution treatment followed by aging can strengthen precipitation-hardened alloys.

Microstructure
Fine precipitates impede dislocation motion and improve high-temperature strength.

Life prediction
Remaining life models combine stress, temperature, exposure time, and damage state.

Characterization
Electron microscopy can reveal precipitate morphology and crack paths.

Comparison
Oxidation resistance and creep strength can trade off when alloying changes phase stability.
